fresh_facts.claim: Operation Epic Fury, a US air, naval, and cyber campaign, targeted over 1,000 Iranian sites in the first 24 hours beginning February 28, 2026, including missile infrastructure and air defenses.
Two US carrier strike groups, USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln, were confirmed in the region as of March 2, 2026 Pentagon briefing.
Four US military deaths were confirmed approximately 57 hours into Operation Epic Fury as of March 2, 2026.
Hundreds of Iranian ballistic missiles and drones were intercepted by US and allied forces (Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, Saudi Arabia) during Operation Epic Fury.
12 F-22 jets were deployed to Israel's Ovda Airbase on February 24, 2026.
14 USAF refueling tankers were deployed at Ben Gurion Airport on February 27, 2026.
USS Gerald R. Ford was positioned off Israel on February 27, 2026.
Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing deployment was reported on March 3, 2026.
A third US carrier, possibly USS Nimitz, was reported to be preparing for deployment on March 7, 2026.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated on March 10, 2026 that renewed talks with the US are unlikely.
President Trump indicated openness to talks with Iran on certain terms on March 10, 2026.
Israeli PM Netanyahu urged Iranians to overthrow the regime on March 10, 2026, via X post.
Israeli President Herzog defended oil site strikes as weakening Iran's war machine without a timetable in a March 10, 2026 Bild interview.
The White House clarified on March 10, 2026 that Trump's 'unconditional surrender' demand means neutralizing Iran's missile and nuclear threats.
Iran's Parliament speaker Qalibaf and IRGC spokesmen rejected ceasefire talks on March 10, 2026 via state media.
The Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader on March 9, 2026 after Ali Khamenei's death on the war's first day.
Reza Pahlavi claimed leadership of a future Iranian transition without mandate or support, reported March 10, 2026.
IDF strikes on March 10, 2026 targeted IRGC, Basij, and security headquarters in Tehran, Tabriz, and Ilam Province, destroying most internal security assets.
An internet blackout in Iran persisted into its 10th day as of March 10, 2026.
Airstrikes on March 9, 2026 hit fuel sites in Tehran and Alborz, causing black rain and triggering oil price increases.
Iran's attacks on regional energy facilities contributed to oil price increases as of March 10, 2026.
A Paris rally on March 7, 2026 supported NCRI's provisional government and Maryam Rajavi's democratic plan.
President Pezeshkian expressed regret for regional attacks and divisions with hardliners on March 7, 2026.
A 2025-2026 internal security crackdown in Iran killed thousands of protesters, prompting US reinforcements announced January 13, 2026.
Failed Oman-mediated talks between the US and Iran occurred on February 6, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury has delivered sustained intensive strikes across Iranian military infrastructure since February 28, with over 1,000 targets hit in the first 24 hours and three carrier strike groups maintaining operational pressure, while the regime faces critical internal divisions following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death on March 5, contested succession of his son Mojtaba amid explicit U.S. rejection of his legitimacy, deepening economic collapse, and persistent internet blackout now in its 10th day.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader on March 9, 2026, following Ali Khamenei's death on March 5, while IDF strikes on March 10 targeted IRGC and internal security headquarters, destroying most regime security assets.
